claims.summary: Huyền Khí is a palace-level strength concept used to judge whether difficult stars are transformed or damage the palace function.
Positive Huyền Khí is used to emphasize strengths; negative Huyền Khí to emphasize weaknesses.
The publisher does not publicly disclose the complete underlying theory and warns against imitation scores.
The displayed whole-chart Huyền Khí total is additive over the twelve displayed palace scores.
claims.evidence: 18/18 manually transcribed summaries match exactly after omitted palaces are treated as zero.

claims.summary: Public Cung Khí pages display 20% palace stem element, 50% palace branch element and 30% palace stem-branch nạp-âm element.
The relationship between Huyền Khí, Xí Hoa and Đẩu Minh is not established by the public sources.
The score alphabet contains many quarter-step values plus smaller residual adjustments, suggesting at least two numeric layers.
claims.evidence: 170/207 observed nonzero palace scores are exact multiples of 0.25.
